title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Satterthwaite, an elderly man traveling from London to Doverton Kingsburn Manor to arrive for tea by four o'clock, experiences mechanical issues with his new car. While his driver and a mechanic negotiate repairs at a service station, Mr. Satterthwaite becomes agitated, making repetitive "clucking" sounds. During this period of restlessness, he recalls seeing something vivid and colorful while driving through the countryside near a rural post office.

The memory involves a connection to a person from his past and various locations, including Corsica and Monte Carlo. Upon remembering the specific trigger, Mr. Satterthwaite rejects his driver's suggestion to take a taxi. Instead, he decides to walk to a location he identifies as the Clown's Cafe. He reaches the village of Kingsburn Darcy and finds the establishment, which is divided into a dining area and a shop selling modern ceramics. The shop window displays a "Rainbow Tea Set" with various colored tea sets, which triggers the realization that the word "Rainbow" was the source of his excitement.

This discovery leads Mr. Satterthwaite to the peculiar hope that he might encounter his old friend, Harry Quinn, in the village. He reflects on his long-standing desire to see Mr. Quinn and his history of befriending influential social figures, such as duchesses, as well as young people in need. Believing that a meeting with Mr. Quinn would signal something significant or destined is about to happen, Mr. Satterthwaite decides to enter the shop to see if there is anything interesting to discover while he waits for his car to be repaired.
